intro: Xanthocercis zambesiaca, commonly known as the Zambian yellow-wood or yellow fever tree, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Africa, to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Xanthocercis zambesiaca include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Xanthocercis zambesiaca is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being made into a tincture or used in topical applications to harness its medicinal benefits.
